definition: The system must detect generic, averaged, over-sanitized outputs and preserve specificity and primitive force.
Authentic captured human audio remains the truth anchor; synthetic audio is exceptional and provenance-flagged.
No edit, generation, or provider materialization job may run before composition role and decision are recorded.
Assets must sequence meaning, attention, proof, and payoff across beats/slides/scenes rather than present disconnected outputs.
Final renders must be reproducible from frozen contracts, assets, seeds, render container versions, and receipts.
A hard gate that blocks privacy, consent, source distortion, synthetic voice, or unsafe claims without over-softening creative edge.
Editing must preserve the non-generic edge product and avoid flattening source meaning into safe centroid advice.
Operator review must show source, lineage, primitive obligations, eval failures, and proposed repair actions.
The system must preserve human expression as the source event; assets are phenotypes of captured expression.
All production begins from human interview activation and expression, not post-hoc prompt generation.
Brand-specific forbidden language, visuals, claims, styles, and emotional postures are explicit constraints.
Human approval is required for production release, publishing, identity usage, synthetic voice exceptions, and locked brand context changes.
All creative contracts are governed by primitive coalitions before archetype, style, or provider selection.
Generated images and B-roll default to real-life references, source nouns, or approved brand assets before abstract invention.
Content must preserve the meaning, provenance, speaker intent, and source timing of the original expression.
Visuals must preserve approved brand aesthetics, recurring visual systems, materiality, and forbidden visual negative space.
High-value visual work must pass through Visual Schema or equivalent research before CAC, GMG, or paper-cut generation.
Copy, captions, audio, and synthetic bridge policies must preserve Positive Space, Negative Space, rhythm, and Emotional DNA.
Set of approved brand photos, footage, objects, avatar references, and identity anchors.
Approved visual identity anchor for the coach/client across realistic, paper-cut, silhouette, or symbolic styles.
Immutable snapshot of brand voice, visual DNA, identity assets, negative space, preferences, and active libraries.
Onboarding workflow that creates the initial creative substrate: identity, acting library, avatar, assets, anchors.
Longitudinal store of approved claims, recurring worldview, performance, audience response, and asset usage.
Brand-specific preference model for restraint, warmth, authority, visual density, humor, materiality, and format choices.
Brand-scoped operational container for context, assets, sessions, editing, publishing, and memory.
Brand-owned identity and context asset library used before broader winning asset libraries.
Permission and product/billing constraints governing which workflows, providers, or exports are available.
Immutable record granting or denying usage scopes for likeness, voice, footage, synthetic assets, and publishing.
Approved likeness and identity reference collection with consent and usage constraints.
Explicitly forbidden language, visual motifs, emotional tones, claims, styles, and identity distortions.
Privileged production user who reviews, approves, revises, exports, and schedules outputs.
Top-level account boundary that owns users, brands, permissions, billing, and audit scope.
Brand-specific social account, platform, format, schedule, caption, and Publer mapping profile.
Permission system for operator, editor, reviewer, publisher, client viewer, and admin actions.
Human actor with roles, permissions, command authority, and audit identity.
Brand-specific visual system: palette, materiality, composition preferences, style routes, forbidden aesthetics.
Brand-specific speech rhythm, vocabulary, emotional amplitude, positive space, negative space, and sonic signature.
State machine governing active, suspended, archived, restored, and inspected workspaces.
Meaning primitive family for body language, acting state, character performance, and delivery posture.
Meaning primitive family for offer clarity, conversion path, business framing, and commercial trust.
Meaning primitive family for story, relational meaning, connection, and narrative arc.
Compressed signature of a selected primitive coalition used to route archetypes, sequence, and composition.
Non-generic, source-specific output of a primitive coalition that preserves force and avoids centroid drift.
Primitive plane containing trigger, friction, feedback, progression, safety, personalization, social, and trust/branding primitives.
Experience primitive family for payoff, validation, progress feedback, scores, and visible closure.
Experience primitive family for cognitive load, ease, clarity, and ability to continue consuming.
Meaning primitive family for humor, benign violation, distortion, irony, meme logic, and social relief.
Primitive plane containing meaning-making, narrative, persuasion, diagnostics, performance, visual, trust, and business primitives.
Experience primitive family for identity recognition, personalization, avatar continuity, and self-recognition.
Experience primitive family for series memory, progression, replay value, and future-value loops.
Meaning primitive family for conviction, contrast, proof, belief shift, and rhetorical force.
Meaning primitive family for internal mechanism, diagnosis, hidden pressure, and psychological interpretation.
A contract-level binding between a primitive and a role such as meaning_transform, visual_guidance, or trust_transfer.
Reasoning pass that proposes possible primitives from context, expression moments, and interview goals.
Canonical contract declaring primary, supporting, suppression, conflict, misuse, and eval obligations for a content unit.
Rule declaring primitives that should not co-activate or must be resolved through CBAR.
Evaluation score indicating whether required primitives were visibly/semantically activated in the output.
Receipt proving coverage, misuse risk, conflict resolution, and evidence for primitive activation.
Registry grouping of primitives such as STR, PRS, HUM, VSG, ACT, TRG, FRC, FBK, PRG, SOC.
Stable identifier for a primitive record used in contracts, evals, and registry lookups.
Risk score that a primitive was overused, applied to the wrong surface, or contradicted source meaning.
Meaning primitive family for proof, testimonial, trust transfer, social validation, and relational credibility.
Experience primitive family for recovery, repair, containment, and hard safety boundaries.
Experience primitive family for participation, sharing, comments, polls, ranking, duels, and referral loops.
Meaning primitive family for structure, compression, form, sequence, and conceptual architecture.
Primitive that must be actively minimized or avoided for a particular source moment or brand.
Experience primitive family for brand trust, recurring identity, future value, and long-term recognition.
Experience primitive family for first-frame entry, hook timing, salience, and initial attention capture.
Meaning primitive family for voice, rhythm, sonic intimacy, audio trust, and verbal presence.
Meaning primitive family for visual direction, annotation, attention path, sonic cues, and comprehension guidance.
Interview/question distillation method for compressing many potential questions into a few high-density ones.
Methodological gate identifying stock visual defaults and replacing them with source-specific visual decisions.
Constraint-Based Adversarial Reasoning method for resolving conflicts between creative goals, primitives, constraints, and doctrine.
Concrete validation checkpoint applying CBAR to a proposed contract, prompt, composition, or provider job.
Combinatorial Controlled Variation method for generating bounded variations without losing primitive force or brand coherence.
Bounded set of creative variations generated by recombining allowed axes while preserving invariants.
Storyboard principle defining what the camera ethically and emotionally believes about the subject.
Method that prevents later stages from changing upstream approved identity, environment, or visual logic.
Principle that specificity creates universality and precise lived details activate recognition.
Pre-prompt adversarial reasoning gate resolving contradictions before visual prompt generation.
Method for forming working assumptions that link guest truth, audience reality, and interviewer resonance.
Layered compression stages that turn research, transcripts, and context into usable creative contracts.
Offline trust and conversion methodology for moving field relationships into recurring value systems.
Single action verb such as gripping, pressing, tracing, releasing, or clenching used to physicalize emotion.
Numbered cinematic light rule selected according to emotional temperature and visual schema.
Tension-selection methodology that protects meaningful edge from polite dilution without manufacturing conflict.
Visual storyboard method capturing Feeling, Body Truth, Environment, Timestamp, and Uniqueness per beat.
Recursive Signal Compression Systems method for distilling noisy context into compressed, operational signals.
System/semantic ontology methodology and registry layer for mapping concepts, dependencies, and domain relations.
System/semantic flow layer or framework registry used for meaning routes, flows, and skill bindings.
Visual taxonomy code for emotional/tonal beat style used in storyboard and visual analyst validation.
Visual hierarchy code for shot/visual power and cinematic function used in storyboard validation.
Visual affect/expression decoder performing semantic check, shadow/body filter, and anti-cliché gate before prompting.
Beat-level interpretation that guides shot type, body truth, environment, and camera behavior.
Compressed visual signal profile representing the specific visual reality, emotion, and aesthetic memory of a project.
Pre-visual agent that builds a researched Visual Schema from transcript, brand avatar, E-Roll, and strategy brief.
Classic visual arrangement suggested by the visual schema for the story or concept.
Process of finding and scoring candidate assets, artifacts, B-roll references, proof objects, and style references.
Existing code contract/class `AssetResearchManifest` discovered in `src/ccp_studio/contracts/visual_research.py`; map into canonical ontology or keep as implementation detail.
Existing code contract/class `AssetResearchReceipt` discovered in `src/ccp_studio/contracts/visual_research.py`; map into canonical ontology or keep as implementation detail.
Research brief capturing the audience’s current anxieties, language, objections, cultural symbols, and unresolved tensions.
Existing code contract/class `CandidateUseMode` discovered in `src/ccp_studio/contracts/visual_research.py`; map into canonical ontology or keep as implementation detail.
Dominant, accent, and avoidance colors drawn from brand avatar, context, and aesthetic memory.
Specific object or environmental detail extracted from transcript or research.
Audience-native object, ritual, brand-coded cue, or social signal that can create recognition.
Peak action moment that can anchor a visual scene or B-roll beat.
External/cultural research used to ground visual schema, cultural DNA, objects, and social context.
Brand-avatar-grounded body/human form information copied verbatim to preserve identity.
Visual rule that a frame should usually express one person, one action, and one emotion.
Research dossier on the guest/client including biography, public language, themes, risks, and story opportunities.
Physical location or spatial context that makes the visual recognizable and grounded.
Existing code contract/class `ImageResolutionMap` discovered in `src/ccp_studio/contracts/visual_research.py`; map into canonical ontology or keep as implementation detail.
Existing code contract/class `LicenseTier` discovered in `src/ccp_studio/contracts/visual_research.py`; map into canonical ontology or keep as implementation detail.
Existing code contract/class `LicensingDecision` discovered in `src/ccp_studio/contracts/visual_research.py`; map into canonical ontology or keep as implementation detail.
Beat-specific light behavior such as harsh, diffused, warm, cold, dirty, golden, flat, or clinical.
Emotionally appropriate light condition such as cold night, golden hour, clinical fluorescent, or window haze.
Transition state or setting such as dusk, waiting room, train, doorway, empty room, or threshold.
Real-world frame such as doorway, window, desk edge, car window, mirror, or shelf used for composition.
Time, texture, genre, or memory flavor such as documentary realism, vintage, editorial, analog archive.
Analysis of published outputs, winning assets, composition performance, fatigue, and audience signal.
Specific emotional texture and micro-expression extracted from strategy and transcript.
Declared relationship between source and generation: direct real reference, composite references, style reference, or abstract exception.
Structured set of source documents, findings, contradictions, and signals used before interview or content planning.
Process of checking source ownership, license, likeness, trademark, privacy, and sensitive-data risks.
Emotion-to-visual distortion mapping such as compression, isolation, weight, expansion, symmetry, or blur.
Everyday human ritual or situation extracted for recognition and accessibility.
Existing code contract/class `VisualCandidate` discovered in `src/ccp_studio/contracts/visual_research.py`; map into canonical ontology or keep as implementation detail.
Existing code contract/class `VisualCandidateScore` discovered in `src/ccp_studio/contracts/visual_research.py`; map into canonical ontology or keep as implementation detail.
Existing code contract/class `VisualResearchQuery` discovered in `src/ccp_studio/contracts/visual_research.py`; map into canonical ontology or keep as implementation detail.
Context-bound visual reality document containing human experience, environment, visual grammar, and aesthetic memory.
A cliché, stock metaphor, or generic visual pattern explicitly forbidden.
A source-grounded visual pattern intentionally allowed because it fits the story and audience.
Detected transcript/source location where an anchor produced useful expression.
Canonical source event capturing interview, transcript, source artifacts, expression moments, and routing evidence.
Working assumption connecting guest truth, audience reality, and interviewer resonance to guide questioning and sequencing.
Follow-up instrument that prevents abstraction and extracts cost, mechanism, scene, or emotional specificity.
Plan of semantic, emotional, proof, visual, and future-value ingredients that the interview should acquire.
Captured unit such as scene, cost, mechanism, quote, proof, memory object, action, or audience question.
Post-session pantry of captured, missing, weak, unexpected, and reusable ingredients with scores and provenance.
Approved source-grounded moment with semantic role, emotional state, primitive fit, and asset compatibility.
Potential source moment detected from transcript, anchors, primitive signals, or emotional density.
Graph relating moments as open question, clue, contradiction, reframe, answer, proof, humanization, or future promise.
Opening phrase/shape that steers a clipable answer and opens the viewer or guest state.
Question-level production contract specifying target state, primitives, anchors, expected ingredients, asset routes, and eval rules.
Primary planning document combining research, strategy, context premises, asset intents, sequence hypotheses, and acquisition plans.
Gate that blocks weak, generic, unsafe, unsupported, or undercovered interview asset contracts.
Order of guest activation states such as rapport, memory, tension, vulnerability, meaning, teaching, and invitation.
Brief capturing the interviewer’s authentic curiosity, personal connection, and questions that feel alive.
Assessment of whether the guest produced a truthful ending, payoff, principle, or unresolved open loop.
Real-time coverage matrix of required ingredients captured, weak, missing, or unexpectedly replaced.
Subtle live assistant that tracks coverage, cues follow-ups, and preserves the interviewer’s presence.
Pre-interview tension map identifying meaningful pressure points, contradictions, costs, and silences.
Audio, video, screenshot, transcript, or external file used as provenance for expression and outputs.
Lineage record proving source, timestamp, rights, consent, and transformation history.
Operational content strategy object containing goals, emotional beats, SPR, target audience, and output intent.
Textual source record with timestamps, speakers, and provenance derived from audio/video.
Time-coded semantic beat structure including pauses, emphasis words, speaker turns, and expression moment bindings.
Output packaging schema such as Scene-to-Principle, Quote Card, Dopamine Cliff Carousel, or Reaction Seed.
Planned set of outputs with source moment, archetype, derivative, CMF route, evaluation, and diversity requirements.
Structured handoff from expression/routing into CMF production for one asset.
Asset showing two models, beliefs, outcomes, or identities in visual opposition.
Reserved code that identifies format, role, route, or package slot for a produced asset.
Group of related assets serving a relationship, proof, teaching, or campaign objective.
Structured viewer-state plan for a single asset derived from primitive coalition and source ingredients.
Meaning structure such as Myth Debunk, Transformation Story, Conceptual Contrast, or Authority Proof.
Desired output form such as vertical video, square soft card, carousel, SuperVisual, or reaction edit.
Explicit cue that this creator or brand will continue to provide useful value over time.
Initial proof package typically containing videos, carousels, meme visuals, polls, and reaction seeds.
Asset that lets the viewer recognize themselves or their group in the content.
Reusable social compression mechanism such as micro-contradiction, relief peak, or tribal recognition.
Recurring content engine turning ongoing sessions into narrative memory, performance learning, and publishing sequence.
Sequence plan across multiple assets in a pack or month to build recognition, value, trust, and action.
Asset whose primary role is credibility, evidence, source validation, or trust transfer.
Participatory response schema such as Validation Reaction, Debate With Jury, Vote Then React, or Reaction Duel.
Recurring content premise that teaches the audience what ongoing value to expect.
Asset designed to invite voting, commenting, ranking, duet/reaction, or sharing.
Narrative asset showing before, disturbance, cost, turning point, realization, and after.
Viewer state where evidence and clues allow anticipation of the answer without confusion.
Video-level sequence of transcript beats with viewer state, timing, visual function, and closure logic.
Requirement specifying how and where an open loop must be answered or transformed.
Operationalized question-anticipation-answer cycle; used as heuristic, not literal biological measurement.
Planned information and visual density pattern across slides or video beats.
Transcript-derived viewer journey concept: stimulation, captivation, anticipation, validation, affection, revelation.
Series/brand-level expectation that this creator will repeatedly solve a problem or provide distinct value.
Messenger-level trust and liking produced by authentic presence, help, warmth, rhythm, and recurring identity.
Evaluation score proving that opened questions were closed truthfully and usefully.
Unresolved question or tension deliberately opened and tracked until closure.
Ordering of assets across a Guest Asset Pack or month to build recognition, trust, and future value.
First-glance state that earns attention through visual salience, motion, contrast, face, artifact, or composition.
Method of revealing information gradually so the viewer predicts and then receives payoff.
Audience-relevant curiosity loop opened by copy, visual contrast, situation, or implied contradiction.
Registry of canonical sequence patterns such as Myth-Mechanism-Replacement or Scene-Cost-Meaning.
Carousel-level plan assigning each slide a viewer state, semantic role, primitive obligation, and composition function.
Source-grounded answer, mechanism, reframe, proof, or action that closes a loop.
Ordered journey through attention, question, prediction, payoff, affinity, and future value.
Program for generating or editing B-roll plates/cards from real-life references, visual schema, and style routes.
Short visual insert or card used inside video, often CAC/GMG/documentary/paper-cut generated from real references.
Declared role an ingredient plays inside composition: hero, proof insert, memory object, foreground prop, card, background.
Production-ready ingredient derived from a source reference and governed by role, style route, frame profile, and primitive binding.
Specific transformed version of an ingredient for a style, frame, materiality, or composition role.
Source-preserving transformation of an approved ingredient for a declared composition role.
Delivery or source frame definition such as 9:16 full vertical, 1:1 soft-rounded editorial, or 4:5 carousel slide.
Provider-produced image/video/layer/plate that remains a candidate until evaluated and approved.
Process of converting ingredients and generated assets into compositable layers with masks, alpha, and metadata.
Structured prompt block such as anchor, contact, composition, atmosphere, imperfection, lens, or negative prompt.
Square or vertical card designed to present screenshot/document/proof evidence with provenance and legibility.
Provider-bound plan containing style route, source references, composition role, required inputs, and blocked/failure rules.
Gate requiring source-grounded references before provider generation for CAC, paper-cut artifact, documentary proof, or GMG 03/04.
1:1 rounded editorial visual/video card with premium edges, shadow, and constrained caption behavior.
Original real-world source such as interview frame, photo, screenshot, object, location, document, or quote.
Selected CAC, GMG, paper-cut, documentary, UI, or avatar route with required inputs and validation rules.
Project-level collection of candidate, approved, missing, and scored visual ingredients.
Beat/scene-bound program tying visual schema, PRIMAL/VAE, codes, style route, frame profile, and composition role.
Context-bound need for a specific class of visual ingredient before composition or provider generation.
Brand/coach library of emotional expression and gesture/body-language states.
Real-life visual artifact such as receipt, screenshot, object, note, ticket, or proof item.
Registry of proof, memory, familiarity, metaphor, micro-semiotic, identity, composition, and style reference classes.
Receipt for intrinsic, contextual, composition, rights, performance, or micro-semiotic evaluation.
Subsystem that ingests, classifies, scores, retrieves, tracks, and learns from creative assets.
Structured query for finding assets by semantic role, primitive fit, visual requirements, rights, and format.
Record of exactly how an asset was used in an output, composition, role, and result.
Brand-scoped storage and metadata system for all approved, candidate, reference, and generated assets.
Reference-only visual library of winning layouts, attention paths, spatial grammars, and density patterns.
Everyday recognizable object that makes a scene feel audience-native.
Record indicating overuse, declining performance, repetition risk, and pause or variant recommendation.
Photo/video/reference proving brand/avatar likeness, physical traits, and identity consistency.
Artifact with narrative, biographical, or emotional continuity value.
Object used to compress an abstract concept into a visible physical representation.
Small culturally loaded object that triggers recognition, belonging, humor, or comments without dominating composition.
Material settings for cut edge, texture, shadow, thickness, jitter, and tactile consistency.
Paper-cut transformed real-life object used for familiarity, proof, metaphor, or scene interaction.
Approved transformed artifact with alpha, mask, materiality, recognizability, and edge-quality scores.
Layered avatar heads, bodies, mouth shapes, expressions, hands, props, rig, and materiality assets.
Post-publication score derived from saves, shares, comments, completion, profile actions, and relative baseline.
Asset whose role is evidence, credibility, documentary proof, or trust transfer.
Record of source, license, usage scope, trademark risk, likeness risk, privacy risk, and restrictions.
Reference-only style/mood/material library used for visual direction but not direct rendering.
Performance-proven asset library promoted from usage and publishing telemetry.
Style family for approved brand avatar performance clips and transparent character layers.
Validation/specialist agent checking CAC prompt compliance, realism, anti-cliché, and source grounding.
Style composer generating Conscious Ambient Cinema prompts using anchor, contact, composition, atmosphere, imperfection, and lens.
Editorial cinematic realism style system grounded in real-life references and structured prompt sections.
Realistic CAC-like visual style for memory, atmosphere, human presence, and emotional B-roll.
Style family for screenshots, documents, evidence cards, receipts, archives, and source-preserving proof.
Validation agent checking GMG expert output compliance, expert isolation, style rules, and source alignment.
Router that chooses GMG expert style rather than writing average prompts directly.
GMG style route for systems, networks, nodes, diagrams, causal flows, and high-end UI motion.
GMG style route for a symbolic high-contrast avatar, weather, noir, single-word impact, and living-statue stance.
GMG style route combining universal stick figures with photorealistic object cutouts on paper texture.
GMG style route for documents, archives, photos, torn artifacts, evidence, tape, paperclips, and stop-motion authenticity.
GMG style route for duotone flat editorial illustrations, metrics, hand-drawn labels, and paper-ground explainers.
GMG style route for black/white geometry, visual equations, negative space, and abstract logic.
Routed motion-graphics style system governed by isolated expert profiles and source-noun extraction.
GMG/Skia/Motion-Canvas-compatible geometric visual style for abstract mechanisms, diagrams, and pure logic.
Style doctrine requiring scenes to route to a specific expert/style rather than blending isolated experts into generic output.
Style route that transforms real-life artifacts into cutout paper objects while preserving recognizability.
Tactile handmade paper-cut visual family for explainers, avatar scenes, and artifact-based familiarity.
Visual style for polls, rankings, tier lists, comments, debate cards, and score states.
Batch structural auditor that authorizes or rejects storyboard batches based on context, identity, and montage logic.
VDP-native visual prompt generation agent that transforms beat clusters into PRIMAL/VAE/constraint-gated prompt files.
Validation agent that verifies storyboard and prompt compliance before provider execution.
Check that light, posture, energy, and emotional direction progress logically across scenes.
Check that Visual Cinematic Premise and camera directive are interpreted rather than invented.
Critical Visual Analyst check that physical DNA remains verbatim and identity does not drift.
Gate preventing downstream prompt changes from violating upstream approved decisions.
Gate resolving transcript, visual schema, and PRIMAL environment conflicts.
Gate separating invariant brand-avatar identity from beat-specific posture and expression.
Gate checking whether lighting preset accidentally evokes a banned stock scene.
Gate ensuring shot choices serve arc beats and visual cinematic premise.
Gate ensuring prompt block lengths and character anchor word counts fit provider behavior.
Check that environment, light, and narrative arc are coherent.
Check that exactly one approved kinetic verb is selected for a beat.
Check that a valid emotional-temperature lighting preset is selected.
Specific bodily manifestation of a beat’s emotion unique to the person/source.
Source-grounded location or spatial environment for the visual beat.
Visceral sentence naming exact emotional frequency for a beat.
Exact quote and timestamp grounding the visual beat.
Reason the scene could only come from this person, not a generic stock subject.
Check that compressed sensory/emotional prompt signature avoids contamination and forbidden terms.
Pre-scored T-Codes, V-Codes, kinetic verbs, beat clusters, and visual intent inputs for storyboard composer.
Visual Analyst check that T-Code matches arc beat and does not violate banned T-Code rules.
Check that quoted source moment and storyboard timestamp match transcript.
Check that the scene is specific rather than generic stock imagery.
Visual Analyst check that high-power V-Codes are used only for appropriate narrative functions.
Stock-scene rejection and replacement with source-specific visual approach.
Specific emotional texture validation before visual prompting.
Body-language filter showing emotion through physical manifestation rather than generic expression.
Horizontal raw/source/reference frame, not a final short-form output in the current strategy.
Square premium frame with rounded inner card, soft shadow, restrained captions, and Apple-like polish.
Instagram/feed poster or carousel slide format with high-save still visual hierarchy.
Full vertical social video or still format optimized for mobile face/object/text hierarchy.
Vertical reaction format with UI/content/prompt on top and human reaction or cutout below.
Planned sequence of visual focus points through a still, slide, or scene.
Multi-slide sequence of slide compositions governed by viewer-state and primitive obligations.
Receipt proving why a composition was selected, what alternatives were rejected, and what roles are locked.
Eval score that final output still matches the locked composition and role assignments.
Eval gate checking archetype fit, primitive alignment, visual ingredient coverage, and composition viability.
Canonical registry of composition templates, families, zones, constraints, and compatible formats.
Reusable spatial grammar for a scene, slide, still, card, or video layout.
Format contract for 9:16, 1:1 soft-rounded, 4:5, or source-only profiles.
Materialized layer list with file refs, alpha/masks, timing, ownership, and render roles.
Deterministic plan of layers, z-index, assets, text, masks, annotations, and materialization jobs.
Composition policy describing where emptiness must be preserved and how density is controlled.
Hand-drawn underline, circle, bracket, arrow, or cross-out used only for semantic emphasis.
Format-specific area preventing text, face, or key object from being cropped or obscured.
Video or visual scene template with layers, timing roles, and render bindings.
Carousel slide template with semantic role, zones, copy budget, and visual hierarchy.
Rounded edge, shadow, inner frame, and premium square-card treatment for 1:1 editorial assets.
Single-image composition program with copy, layers, assets, style route, and render contract.
Rules for headline, subtitles, support copy, labels, CTA, and quote overlays.
Timeline-based visual composition using footage, b-roll, captions, overlays, artifacts, and camera behavior.

Reusable character performance engine producing transparent avatar clips/layers for compositions.
Engine that onboards a brand and creates identity, acting, avatar, asset, and anchor libraries.
Primitive-governed sequence compiler producing multi-slide carousels from ingredients and composition templates.
Engine that compiles viewer-state and package sequences from primitives and ingredients.
Engine that plans, tracks, and inventories ingredients acquired through interviews.
Engine/workbench for research, context premises, interview contracts, edging, and live acquisition.
Engine admitting, governing, querying, projecting, and using brand, asset, primitive, and performance memory.
Registry/glossary system managing canonical names, layers, dependencies, migration actions, and code refs.
Engine for real-life artifact classification, paper-cut transformation, animation behavior, and usage scoring.
Engine that schedules provider jobs, retries, receipts, worker routes, and blocked provider constraints.
Engine managing publishing intent, Publer job, platform captions, schedules, and post telemetry.
Operator review, approval, revision, blocker, evidence, and Telegram quick-review system.
Lower-level still image routing and Skia composition engine used by SuperVisuals and feed posts.
Router selecting CAC, GMG, paper-cut, documentary, UI, or avatar style route based on ingredient and primitive.
Engine for single-image and still visual production from context, ingredients, composition, style route, render, and eval.
Primitive-governed timeline compiler producing four video formats, reframing, captions, b-roll, audio, and render contracts.
Pre-production engine that builds Visual Schema and visual ingredient requirements.
UI for avatar rig, expressions, acting states, paper-cut materiality, and character performance previews.
UI for asset vaults, paper-cut artifacts, anchors, rights, evals, winning assets, and fatigue.
UI for sequence, slide roles, composition choices, assets, copy, layers, evals, and export.
UI for deciding keep, extend, wrap, merge, split, replace, or deprecate objects.
UI for searching terms, layers, dependencies, code refs, bundle refs, and migration actions.
Primary UI shell with context lineage, workbench, inspector/agent, and execution trace.
UI for captions, schedules, Publer jobs, platform status, and performance feedback.
UI for single-image production: context, research, composition, layers, assets, prompts, render, eval, approval.
UI for transcript beats, timeline, reframing, b-roll, captions, audio, evals, and render review.
UI for Visual Schema, PRIMAL, VAE, storyboard, Visual Analyst, and Storyboard Commander.
Node-based self-hosted generative workflow executor for image/video/asset generation pipelines.
Media finishing tool for muxing, codecs, scaling, audio mix, loudness, and export variants.
Image editing provider used for object/identity-preserving edits, inpainting, style repairs, and integration cleanup.
Image generation/editing provider used for onboarding assets, character/object sheets, paper-cut props, and repairs.
Composition-oriented image generation provider used for layout/concept plates, typography-like arrangement, and visual ideation.
Automated visual quality/evaluation model candidate for asset and final-render QC.
Speech restoration/enhancement provider for raw interview audio and voice output quality equalization.
Voice generation/voice-clone provider candidate for approved voice DNA boost inserts.
TypeScript animation/choreography tool for diagrams, procedural motion, and character/scene choreography.
Publishing provider for upload, scheduling, posting, job status, and analytics ingestion.
Layer decomposition model/provider for turning flat images into editable RGBA layer stacks.
React/TypeScript deterministic video composition and render framework.
Viseme cue generation fallback for 2D mouth-shape animation.
Remote API execution environment for ComfyUI-style workflows and batch jobs.
Segmentation/masking/tracking provider for people, objects, cutouts, and background removal.
Specific-scene/motion-transfer style reference for dance/meme/video imitation workflows.
Single-image character layer decomposition reference system for semantically separated PSD-like body layers.
Deterministic 2D rendering/compositing surface for still visuals, geometry, text, cards, and layers.
2D skeletal animation runtime/interchange target with bones, slots, skins, attachments, and animation tracks.
2D puppet authoring reference for PSD import, rigging, mesh deformation, shape keys, timeline, and export.
Operator command, notification, approval, and agent chat surface.
Lightweight preview, chat, review, approve/reject/revise mobile surface inside Telegram.
Transcript alignment, word-level timestamp, diarization/forced-alignment provider.
Motion plan for layers, paper-cut objects, characters, diagrams, and transitions.
